title: oxalis kollmannii
intro: Oxalis kollmannii, also known as the purple wood-sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae), native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of oxalic acid. Oxalis kollmannii has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and potentially helping to manage blood sugar levels and cholesterol levels. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
